zotero.abstractNote: The goal of disentangling the underlying causal factors of data is a central challenge in machine learning. We propose a new objective for learning to disentangle causal mechanisms, based on the principle that a causal model should be robust to changes in the environment or task. We formulate this as a meta-learning problem, where the goal is to learn a representation that allows for fast adaptation to new tasks by leveraging the underlying causal structure. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on several synthetic and real-world datasets, showing that it can learn to disentangle causal factors more effectively than existing methods.
